chapter_voice_summary: BG 2.24 holds the chapter's didactic / corrective register. The nine-property checklist is presented as a concrete audit artefact, not as inspirational doctrine. The fintech API governance scenario uses Maya, Idris, and Ravi without sentimentalising them; their work is opening a spreadsheet and pasting a checklist. Maya's closing line — 'Krishna's list. Run any candidate against it; the ones that pass are the ones the company can actually build on. Nobody laughs — the property list landed' — is in the chapter's senior-engineer-voice. Diction is technical (uncleavable, incombustible, primordial) without slipping into mysticism. Tone consistent across 2.21-2.24. PASS.
